text: ‘A bastard born’; the word in the Hebrew text is of uncertain meaning, and some would translate simply ‘a champion’.

It seems clear, from what is said here, that verses 21 and 22 of chapter sixteen were written by way of anticipation, describing what was to happen later on. It was not until after the combat with Goliath that Saul took any notice of the boy who had played the harp, now and again, in his presence.
